title: STANDARD FOR SORGHUM FLOUR CXS 173-1989 Adopted in 1989. Revised in 1995. Amended in 2019, 2023.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR SORGHUM FLOUR CXS 173-1989 Adopted in 1989. Revised in 1995. Amended in 2019, 2023.
sections.summary: Title and history: Standard for Sorghum Flour (CXS 173-1989), adopted 1989, revised 1995, amended 2019 and 2023.
Notice that amendments were made to the Annex following decisions at the Forty-sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023.
This standard applies to sorghum flour intended for direct human consumption (per Section 2.1) and explicitly excludes grits or meal obtained from Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench.

Sorghum flour is defined as the product from Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench obtained by industrial milling in which the seed coat is removed, the germ is largely removed, and the endosperm is comminuted to a suitable fineness.

Sorghum flour must be safe and suitable for human consumption, free from abnormal flavours, odours, living insects and filth that may hazard health; specific compositional limits include moisture content maximum 15% m/m (with lower limits possible for certain destinations) and tannin content not to exceed 0.3% on a dry matter basis.

Sorghum flour shall be safe and suitable for human consumption and free from abnormal flavours, odours, living insects and filth (impurities of animal origin, including dead insects) in amounts which may represent a hazard to human health.

sections.section: 3.1.1 Sorghum flour shall be safe and suitable for human consumption.
sections.summary: Sorghum flour must be safe and suitable for human consumption.

sections.section: 3.1.2 Sorghum flour shall be free from abnormal flavours, odours, and living insects.
sections.summary: Requires sorghum flour to be free from abnormal flavours, odours and living insects.

sections.section: 3.1.3 Sorghum flour shall be free from filth (impurities of animal origin, including dead insects) in amounts which may represent a hazard to human health.
sections.summary: Prohibits filth (impurities of animal origin, including dead insects) in sorghum flour at levels that may represent a hazard to human health.

Sets specific quality factors for sorghum flour, including a maximum moisture content (15% m/m, with possible lower limits for certain destinations based on climate/transport/storage) and a tannin limit (≤0.3% on a dry matter basis).
Sets a maximum moisture content for sorghum flour of 15% m/m and advises that lower moisture limits may be required for some destinations depending on climate and transport/storage duration.
Limits the tannin content of sorghum flour to no more than 0.3% on a dry matter basis.

Addresses contaminants: sorghum flour must be free from heavy metals at hazardous levels and must comply with Codex-established maximum residue/mycotoxin limits for pesticides and mycotoxins applicable to this commodity.
Requires sorghum flour to be free from heavy metals in amounts that may represent a hazard to human health.
Requires sorghum flour to comply with the Codex Alimentarius Commission's maximum pesticide residue limits established for this commodity.
Requires sorghum flour to comply with the Codex Alimentarius Commission's maximum mycotoxin limits established for this commodity.

Recommends handling sorghum flour in accordance with Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene and other relevant CAC codes, requires the product to be free from objectionable matter under good manufacturing practice, and when tested must not contain microorganisms, parasites or microbial-derived substances at levels that pose a health hazard.

sections.section: 5.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General principles of food hygiene (CXC 1-1969), 1  and other codes of practice recommended by the CAC which are relevant to this product.
sections.summary: Recommends that sorghum flour be prepared and handled in accordance with the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and other relevant CAC codes of practice.

sections.section: 5.2 To the extent possible in good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter.
sections.summary: Requires sorghum flour, to the extent achievable by good manufacturing practice, to be free from objectionable matter.

sections.section: 5.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product:
sections.summary: When tested by appropriate sampling and examination, sorghum flour must be free from microorganisms and parasites and must not contain substances originating from microorganisms at levels that may represent a health hazard.

Specifies packaging requirements: containers must protect the hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities of sorghum flour; packaging materials must be safe and not impart toxic substances or undesirable odours/flavours; and sacks must be clean, sturdy and securely sewn or sealed.

sections.section: 6.1 Sorghum flour shall be packaged in containers which will safeguard the hygienic, nutritional, technological, and organoleptic qualities of the product.
sections.summary: Requires sorghum flour to be packaged in containers that safeguard its hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities.

sections.section: 6.2 The containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their  intended  use.  They  should  not  impart  any  toxic  substance  or  undesirable  odour  or  flavour  to  the product.
sections.summary: Requires packaging materials and containers to be safe and suitable for intended use and not to impart any toxic substances, undesirable odours or flavours to sorghum flour.

sections.section: 6.3 When the product is packaged in sacks, these must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.
sections.summary: Requires sacks used for sorghum flour to be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.

Adds labelling provisions: the product name must be 'sorghum flour', and non-retail containers must bear or be accompanied by the product name, lot identification and the manufacturer/packer's name and address (or an identifiable mark linked to accompanying documents).
Requires the name of the product on the label to be 'sorghum flour'.

Specifies that methods of analysis and sampling for checking compliance with this standard shall be those in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions.

Annex provides compositional and quality limits and method references for the product (ASH 0.9–1.5% d.m.; PROTEIN ≥8.5% d.m.; CRUDE FAT 2.2–4.7% d.m.; CRUDE FIBRE ≤1.8% d.m.; COLOUR 18–30 units; PARTICLE SIZE—100% passing 0.5 mm for 'fine' or 1 mm for 'medium'), advises users to specify which limit and analytic method when multiple are given, refers several tests to Section 8, and lists bibliographic references.
